sections.heading: Atlas Administration API
sections.body:
Programmatically perform administrative tasks on your Atlas
cluster outside of the Atlas UI with the Atlas Administration API.
Atlas Administration API resources add, edit, or delete administrative
objects within Atlas, including projects, users, and
clusters.

You can explore the various endpoints available through the Atlas Administration API
in MongoDB's [Postman workspace](https://www.postman.com/mongodb-devrel/workspace/mongodb-atlas-administration-apis/overview).

To configure access for the Atlas Administration API, see [API Access Lists endpoint](atlas-admin-api-access).

> **See Also:** 
>
> - [Atlas Administration API](atlas-admin-api).
>
> - [See the Atlas Admin API Specification](https://mongodb.com/docs/atlas/reference/api-resources-spec/v2).

sections.heading: Atlas Go SDK (Software Development Kit)
sections.body:
The Atlas Go SDK (Software Development Kit) provides platform-specific and Go language-specific
tools, libraries, and documentation to help you build applications that
interact with Atlas quickly and easily using the Atlas Administration API.

> **See Also:** [Go SDK](atlas-admin-sdk).

sections.heading: Atlas Embedding and Reranking API
sections.body:
The Embedding and Reranking API provides endpoints for accessing Voyage AI's
state-of-the-art embedding and reranking models. Use the REST API
and available client SDKs to power search and retrieval in your AI applications.

<Card cta="Learn about the Embedding and Reranking API" url="https://www.mongodb.com/docs/voyageai/api-reference/overview/" />

sections.heading: Atlas CLI
sections.body:
The Atlas CLI is a command-line interface built specifically for
MongoDB Atlas. You can use Atlas CLI to interact with Atlas
from the terminal with short and intuitive commands and accomplish
complex operational tasks such as creating a cluster or setting up an
access list interactively and programmatically.

> **See Also:** 
>
> - [Learn more about the Atlas CLI](atlas/cli/current).
>
> - [Get Started with the Atlas CLI](atlas/cli/current/atlas-cli-getting-started).

sections.heading: HashiCorp Terraform MongoDB Atlas Provider
sections.body:
HashiCorp Terraform MongoDB Atlas Provider provides refined
programmatic access and management of MongoDB Atlas resources. You
can define your environment using the HashiCorp Configuration Language (HCL)
and deploy, update, and manage Atlas infrastructure as code through
HashiCorp Terraform. This allows you to integrate Atlas into your
continuous delivery workflows.

> **See Also:** 
>
> - [Learn more about MongoDB Atlas and HashiCorp Terraform](https://www.mongodb.com/atlas/hashicorp-terraform).
>
> - [Managing IaC with MongoDB Atlas and HashiCorp Terraform](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zoMB6mlq46k) (Video Walkthrough).

sections.heading: Cloud Development Kit for Terraform
sections.body:
CDK (Cloud Development Kit) for Terraform leverages the
MongoDB Atlas Atlas Administration API to enable provisioning across
popular programming languages such as JavaScript, TypeScript, Python,
Java, Go, and C#. Utilizing the powerful capabilities of Terraform,
with CDKTF (Cloud Development Kit for Terraform) you can now
create, manage, and update your Atlas resources with ease and deploy
them seamlessly in the cloud without context switching from the native
programming language of choice.

> **See Also:** 
>
> - [MongoDB Cloud Development Kit for Terraform](https://constructs.dev/search?q=\&cdk=cdktf\&keywords=terraform\&offset=0).
>
> - [Managing IaC with MongoDB Atlas and the Terraform CDK](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9f0WghCYsEw) (Video Walkthrough).

sections.heading: MongoDB Atlas AWS CloudFormation Resources
sections.body:
Atlas CloudFormation Resources helps you to manage
MongoDB Atlas. It simplifies provisioning and management of
Atlas features on AWS (Amazon Web Services). You can create YAML- or JSON-based templates
for the service or application architectures you want and have AWS (Amazon Web Services)
CloudFormation use those templates for quick and reliable provisioning
of the services or applications (called "stacks"). You can also easily
update or replicate the stacks as needed.

> **See Also:** 
>
> - [Learn more about Atlas CloudFormation](https://www.mongodb.com/atlas/aws-cloudformation).
>
> - [Atlas CloudFormation Resources](https://github.com/mongodb/mongodbatlas-cloudformation-resources/).

sections.heading: AWS Partner Solutions
sections.body:
AWS (Amazon Web Services) Partner Solutions provide pre-built CloudFormation templates to
help you provision a group of Atlas resources for specific use cases
instead of deploying them one by one. Partner Solutions help you deploy
popular technologies to AWS (Amazon Web Services) according to AWS (Amazon Web Services) best practices.

> **See Also:** [AWS Quick Starts](https://aws.amazon.com/quickstart/?solutions-all.sort-by=item.additionalFields.sortDate\&solutions-all.sort-order=desc\&awsf.filter-content-type=*all\&awsf.filter-tech-category=*all\&awsf.filter-industry=*all\&solutions-all.q=mongodb\&solutions-all.q_operator=AND).

sections.heading: AWS Cloud Development Kit
sections.body:
If you prefer using familiar programming languages like JavaScript,
TypeScript, Python, Java, C#, and Go rather than using YAML or JSON (Javascript Object Notation),
you can use the AWS (Amazon Web Services) CDK (Cloud Development Kit) to deploy
Atlas resources. When you execute your AWS (Amazon Web Services) CDK (Cloud
Development Kit) applications, they automatically generate
CloudFormation templates, which in turn utilize the AWS (Amazon Web Services) CloudFormation
service for resource provisioning. This way, you benefit from the
abstraction and flexibility provided by AWS (Amazon Web Services) CDK (Cloud
Development Kit) and take advantage of the robustness of CloudFormation
for infrastructure management, while still using the programming
language of your choice.

> **See Also:** 
>
> - [Atlas AWS Cloud Development Kit](https://github.com/mongodb/awscdk-resources-mongodbatlas/blob/main/examples/README.md).
>
> - [How to Deploy Atlas with AWS CDK](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=p7Fru0fbmxY) (Video Walkthrough).

sections.heading: Atlas Kubernetes Operator
sections.body:
The Atlas Kubernetes Operator enables you to manage Atlas resources through custom resources that
you apply in Kubernetes. This allows an infrastructure as code approach to manage
declarative configuration for Atlas using a GitOps workflow, allowing developers
to configure Atlas resources using the same process and tooling that they use to
deploy application components into Kubernetes. The automatic creation of a Kubernetes secret
containing the connection string for a database user simplifies the process of
connecting an application to the database.

> **See Also:** 
>
> - [Learn more about the Atlas Kubernetes Operator](atlas/operator/current).
>
> - [Quick Start Tutorial](atlas/operator/current/ak8so-quick-start).
